2606.27598.title: Narrative-UFET: Narrative Generation for Ultra-Fine Entity Typing
2606.27598.paper_summary: Ultra-fine entity typing (UFET) assigns highly specific types to entity mentions, but current approaches struggle with types in the long tail. We hypothesize that a key limitation is the reliance on sentence-level context, since disambiguating evidence is often spread across multiple sentences. Testing this has been difficult because all existing UFET resources are sentence-level. We present Narrative-UFET, a controlled extension of UFET in which each entity mention is paired with an automatically generated short, coherent narrative. Synthesizing narratives lets us isolate the effect of specific discourse properties. We experiment with two paired variants: one in which the entity's type is held constant across the narrative (Maintain) and one in which it shifts (Change). We show that narrative context yields consistent improvements on long-tail types over sentence-level baselines, with the Change variant providing the stronger signal. A comparison against naturally occurring contexts shows that synthetic narratives yield stronger gains, indicating that controlled discourse construction can surface signals that real text leaves implicit. Substantial room for improvement remains, suggesting open directions in both discourse modeling and narrative construction.
2606.27598.paper_summary_zh: 超細粒度實體類型化（UFET）為實體提及分配高度特定的類型，但當前的方法在長尾類型上面臨困難。我們假設一個關鍵限制是依賴句子級別的上下文，因為消歧義的證據通常分散在多個句子中。測試這一點一直很困難，因為所有現有的UFET資源都是句子級別的。我們提出Narrative-UFET，這是一個受控的UFET擴展，其中每個實體提及都與自動生成的短小且連貫的敘事配對。合成敘事使我們能夠孤立特定話語特性的影響。我們實驗了兩個配對變體：一個在其中實體的類型在敘事中保持不變（Maintain），另一個則會改變（Change）。我們顯示，敘事上下文在長尾類型上對句子級基準產生了一致的改善，其中Change變體提供了更強的信號。與自然出現的上下文進行比較顯示，合成敘事帶來了更強的增益，這表明受控的話語構建可以揭示真實文本隱含的信號。仍然有相當大的改進空間，這表明在話語建模和敘事構建方面有開放的方向。
2606.27598.author: Mreedul Gupta et.al.
2606.27598.authors: Mreedul Gupta, Advait Deshmukh, Ashwin Umadi, Matt Pauk, Maria Leonor Pacheco
